title: COINTELPRO Black Extremists — Part 01
description: FBI HQ counterintelligence file targeting 'Black Nationalist - Hate Groups' under COINTELPRO. Master file documenting FBI's operations against the Black Panther Party, RAM, Republic of New Afrika, SCLC, SNCC, and individuals including Martin Luther King Jr., Stokely Carmichael, Eldridge Cleaver, and Huey Newton.

title: COINTELPRO Black Extremists — Part 08
description: FBI HQ COINTELPRO file Part 8, including memoranda authorizing Director-level disruption ops against Black Panther figures who became political prisoners (Geronimo Pratt, Huey Newton, Panther 21).

title: COINTELPRO Black Extremists — Part 10
description: Continued FBI COINTELPRO Black Extremists file, mid-program operations. Documents FBI dirty-tricks campaigns referenced in later wrongful-conviction litigation for Geronimo Pratt and Dhoruba bin Wahad.

title: COINTELPRO New Left HQ — Part 01
description: FBI Headquarters file on the COINTELPRO program against the New Left. Surveillance/disruption file underlying federal cases against SDS, the Weather Underground, and the Chicago 8.

title: COINTELPRO Socialist Workers Party — Part 01 of 05
description: FBI HQ COINTELPRO file targeting the Socialist Workers Party. Documents ten years of warrantless surveillance the SWP successfully sued the FBI over (Socialist Workers v. Attorney General, 1986).

title: COINTELPRO Socialist Workers Party — Part 05 (Final)
description: Final volume of the COINTELPRO-SWP HQ file. Closing memoranda on the SWP disruption program.

title: COINTELPRO Puerto Rican Groups — Part 1 of 11
description: FBI HQ COINTELPRO file on 'Groups Seeking Independence for Puerto Rico.' Surveillance file underlying prosecutions of Nationalist Party members and FALN/Macheteros prisoners including Oscar López Rivera.

title: COINTELPRO White Hate Groups — Part 01
description: FBI COINTELPRO file on KKK and white-supremacist groups. Includes informant relationships with klansmen later prosecuted in the Viola Liuzzo and civil rights worker murders.

title: Martin Luther King, Jr. — Part 1 of 2
description: FBI HQ file on Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., released under FOIA. Surveillance file documenting wiretaps, bugs, and the 'suicide letter' campaign against King, the most famous American victim of COINTELPRO.

title: Martin Luther King, Jr. — Part 2 of 2
description: Second portion of the MLK HQ file. Continuation of FBI surveillance file on King through 1968.

title: Malcolm X — Part 01 of 38
description: First part of a 38-part Malcolm X (Malcolm Little) FBI HQ file. Surveillance file on Malcolm X, target of COINTELPRO operations and Nation of Islam infiltration relevant to ongoing exoneration cases (Aziz, Islam).

title: Malcolm X — Part 20 of 38
description: Mid-section of the FBI Malcolm X HQ file; includes post-assassination investigation references.

title: Malcolm X — Part 33 of 38
description: Later section of the FBI Malcolm X file. Includes informant material relevant to the wrongful convictions of Muhammad Aziz and Khalil Islam.

title: Black Panther Party — Part 01 of 34
description: FBI Charlotte field-office file on the Black Panther Party. Surveillance/disruption file on the BPP — the organization whose members produced the largest cluster of U.S. political prisoners (Pratt, bin Wahad, Acoli, Bukhari, Balagoon, Sundiata, Russell Maroon Shoatz).

title: Black Panther Party — Part 06 of 34
description: FBI BPP file Part 6. Same BPP surveillance series, additional volume.

title: Fred Hampton — Part 01
description: FBI HQ file on Fred Hampton, Chairman of the Illinois Black Panther Party. Documents the FBI / Chicago PD targeting that culminated in the police killing of Hampton and Mark Clark on December 4, 1969.

title: Fred Hampton — Part 2 of 2
description: Second part of the Fred Hampton FBI file. Surveillance leading to Hampton's killing.

title: Stokely Carmichael — Part 01
description: FBI HQ file on Stokely Carmichael (Kwame Ture), SNCC and Black Panther leader. COINTELPRO target; file contains memoranda on disruption tactics and travel monitoring.

title: Stokely Carmichael — Part 05 (Final)
description: Final part of the FBI Stokely Carmichael file.

title: Weather Underground (Weathermen) — Part 1 of 6
description: FBI HQ file on the Weather Underground Organization. Surveillance/manhunt file underlying federal cases against WUO members including David Gilbert, Kathy Boudin, Judith Clark, and Marilyn Buck.

title: Weather Underground (Weathermen) — Part 6 of 6 (Final)
description: Final volume of WUO FBI file; includes Brink's-era references for Gilbert/Boudin prosecutions.

title: Viola Liuzzo — Part 1 of 17
description: FBI file on the Klan murder of civil rights worker Viola Liuzzo. FBI informant Gary Rowe was in the car when Liuzzo was killed; the file is core evidence in COINTELPRO 'White Hate Groups' accountability.

title: Cesar Chavez — Part 1 of 17
description: FBI HQ file on Cesar Chavez and the United Farm Workers. White-House-directed 'special investigation' surveillance of Chavez; relevant to labor-organizer prosecution history.

title: Cesar Chavez — Part 2 of 17
description: FBI Chavez file Part 2.

title: MOVE — FBI File
description: FBI file on the MOVE organization, Philadelphia. Surveillance file on MOVE — whose members (MOVE 9, Mumia Abu-Jamal supporters) are core U.S. political prisoners — covering the period of the 1978 Powelton Village confrontation.

title: Kathy Boudin — Part 01
description: FBI HQ file on Kathy Boudin, Weather Underground member. Surveillance and fugitive file on Boudin, prosecuted for the 1981 Brink's robbery alongside David Gilbert, Mutulu Shakur, and Sekou Odinga.

title: Jean Seberg — FBI File
description: FBI file on actress Jean Seberg, a BPP financial supporter. COINTELPRO smear campaign against Seberg for her support of the Panthers, including planting a false rumor that she was pregnant by a Panther — an emblematic COINTELPRO case.

title: George Lester Jackson — FBI File
description: FBI HQ file on George L. Jackson, Soledad Brother and BPP Field Marshal. Surveillance of George Jackson up to his killing at San Quentin on August 21, 1971; directly tied to the Soledad Brothers and San Quentin 6 prosecutions.

title: Church Committee Final Report — Book III
description: Book III: Supplementary Detailed Staff Reports on Intelligence Activities and the Rights of Americans. The definitive congressional documentation of COINTELPRO operations against MLK, BPP, AIM, SWP, Puerto Rican independence groups, and the Communist Party USA.
publisher: U.S. Senate Select Committee on Intelligence Activities

title: Church Committee Final Report — Book II
description: Book II: Intelligence Activities and the Rights of Americans. Lays out the FBI's domestic intelligence and disruption programs against civil rights and antiwar movements; named source for many political-prisoner wrongful-conviction claims.
publisher: U.S. Senate Select Committee on Intelligence Activities

title: Church Committee Hearings Vol. 6 — Federal Bureau of Investigation
description: Senate hearings on FBI intelligence activities. Testimony documenting FBI operations against political prisoners (King, BPP, SDS); foundational for COINTELPRO-era PP cases.

title: Church Committee Hearings Vol. 7 — Covert Action
description: Senate hearings on covert action. Companion to the FBI volume; addresses CIA and FBI surveillance overlap relevant to MLK and antiwar prosecutions.

title: Church Committee Hearings Vol. 2 — Huston Plan
description: Senate hearings on the Huston Plan for expanded domestic intelligence. Documents the Nixon-era plan that authorized illegal surveillance against Weather Underground, BPP, and antiwar-movement prisoners.

title: FBI File on the Black Panther Party (NC Subfile 105-165706)
description: Fifteen-section FBI HQ subfile on BPP activity in North Carolina. Same BPP surveillance series as the Vault entries above.

title: FBI File on Russell Means
description: FBI file on AIM co-founder Russell Means. Surveillance file on Means around Wounded Knee 1973; directly tied to AIM political-prisoner cases including Leonard Peltier.

title: FBI File on John Trudell
description: FBI file on AIM leader and Radio Free Alcatraz broadcaster John Trudell. Documents FBI targeting of Trudell that some argue connects to the 1979 arson death of his family; AIM context for Peltier.

title: FBI Investigation File on Communist Infiltration of the SCLC
description: FBI 'COMINFIL' file on the Southern Christian Leadership Conference. Surveillance pretext file used to justify wiretapping King and SCLC organizers.

title: FBI New York City Field Office File on Malcolm X
description: NYC field office file on Malcolm X. Local complement to the HQ file; relevant to the Aziz / Islam exoneration findings.

title: Eugene V. Debs — Canton Speech (1918)
description: Debs's June 16, 1918 antiwar speech and trial record under the Espionage Act. Debs is the foundational U.S. Espionage Act political prisoner (10-year sentence, Atlanta federal pen); he ran for president from prison in 1920.

title: A Fragment of the Prison Experiences of Emma Goldman and Alexander Berkman
description: Pamphlet documenting Goldman and Berkman's imprisonment at Jefferson City and Atlanta for anti-conscription organizing. Directly about two of the most famous U.S. political prisoners; both convicted under the 1917 Espionage Act and deported in 1919.

title: Paul Avrich Anarchism Collection — Finding Aid
description: Finding aid to the Paul Avrich anarchism collection at the Library of Congress, listing imprisoned anarchists' pamphlets. Index to LoC holdings on Sacco-Vanzetti, Berkman, Goldman, Haymarket Martyrs and other anarchist political prisoners.

title: The Autobiography of Big Bill Haywood
description: Haywood's posthumous autobiography covering the Western Federation of Miners, the IWW, and his 1918 Espionage Act conviction. Haywood was the lead IWW defendant convicted with 100+ Wobblies in the 1918 Chicago mass trial under the Espionage Act — a foundational U.S. PP case.

title: Solidarity Forever: An Oral History of the IWW
description: Oral history interviews with IWW members including Espionage-Act-era veterans by Stewart Bird, Dan Georgakas, and Deborah Shaffer. Firsthand accounts from IWW prisoners and defendants of the 1917-1919 federal raids and trials.

title: FBI Files on Cesar Chavez (1966-1975) — Consolidated
description: Consolidated PDF of FBI HQ files on Chavez and the United Farm Workers.

title: FBI File on Stokely Carmichael — Consolidated
description: Consolidated FBI Stokely Carmichael file. Carmichael was a primary COINTELPRO target.

title: FBI COINTELPRO Puerto Rico — Consolidated
description: Consolidated COINTELPRO Puerto Rico HQ file. Surveillance file underlying Nationalist Party and FALN prosecutions (Lolita Lebrón, Rafael Cancel Miranda, Oscar López Rivera).

title: FBI COINTELPRO Socialist Workers Party — Consolidated
description: Consolidated FBI COINTELPRO-SWP file. Documents ten years of warrantless FBI surveillance/disruption of the SWP later ruled illegal in federal court.

title: Agents of Repression: The FBI's Secret Wars Against the Black Panther Party and the American Indian Movement
description: Ward Churchill and Jim Vander Wall's documentary history of FBI operations against the BPP and AIM. Primary published synthesis of COINTELPRO targeting of Peltier, Pratt, the Panther 21, and Wounded Knee defendants.
authors: Ward Churchill and Jim Vander Wall

title: NARA SF-115 Records Schedule N1-060-02-004 — COINTELPRO and Related FBI Files
description: NARA records schedule for FBI COINTELPRO and related domestic-intelligence files transferred to NARA RG 65. Authoritative index to which COINTELPRO/FBI political-surveillance files have been transferred to NARA and are accessible for research.
